hero_description: Dpowcoin Core is an open-source cryptocurrency implementing the Argon2id proof-of-work algorithm — ASIC-resistant, memory-hard, and built for fair mining.
home_feat1_body: Memory-hard proof-of-work resistant to ASIC mining. Fair distribution through CPU accessibility.
home_feat2_body: Fully open-source under the MIT license. Auditable, forkable, community-driven development on GitHub.
home_feat3_body: Deterministic builds via GNU Guix. Every binary is independently verifiable. GPG-signed release attestations.

verify_intro: Always verify the SHA256 checksum and GPG signature before running any binary.
verify_guix_body: Dpowcoin Core is built reproducibly using GNU Guix. Builder attestations and keys are published at
wallets_web_lead: Browser-based wallet — no installation required. Your keys stay on your device.
wallets_web_warning: This is a non-custodial web wallet. Never share your seed phrase or private keys with anyone.
mining_lead: Dpowcoin uses the Argon2id proof-of-work algorithm — mineable with CPU.
mining_solo_lead: Mine directly to your own full node. You keep 100% of block rewards.
mining_step2_desc: Windows and Linux builds — choose the binary that matches your CPU:
mining_pools_lead: Pool mining gives you steady payouts. Connect using stratum settings shown on each pool's dashboard.
resources_lead: Tools, explorers, exchanges, and community links for Dpowcoin.
dev_contribute_body: Dpowcoin Core is an open contributor project — anyone is welcome to contribute via peer review, testing, and patches. There is no privileged developer class; contributors earn trust through merit. Repository maintainers are responsible for merging pull requests and the release cycle.
dev_rpc_intro: Complete JSON-RPC API reference for dpowcoin-cli and the RPC interface.
footer_rights: Open-source software, released under the MIT License.
spec_subtitle: Full technical parameters of the Dpowcoin (DPC) network
spec_pow_note: Block hash: salt = SHA-512(SHA-512(header)); h1 = Argon2id(header, salt, m=4MB, t=2, p=2); PoW = Argon2id(header, salt=h1, m=32MB, t=2, p=2). Two chained Argon2id passes, salted by a double SHA-512 digest of the header — memory-hard and ASIC-resistant by design.
spec_addr_note: Version bytes shown as decimal and hex. All address types are 34 characters.
vest_subtitle: Yespower is being dropped from consensus at block 225,000 — Dpowcoin becomes Argon2id-only going forward.
vest_launch_body: Before block 225,000, Dpowcoin validated every header with two independent proof-of-work checks sharing one target: hash = { Yespower(header), Argon2id(header) }, valid ⇔ both hashes ≤ target(nBits). Yespower was checked first as a cheap filter; Argon2id was only computed if Yespower passed.
vest_protect_body: Because both algorithms shared the same target, Yespower added no real security beyond what Argon2id already provides, while doubling the work LWMA has to account for and complicating the codebase for no measurable benefit to the network. Since the two checks are independent, dropping Yespower does not change difficulty adjustment or chain weight — both are fully algorithm-agnostic. The Dual PoW idea was worth trying, but this implementation created more problems than it solved. The “Dual PoW” name is kept for historical reasons only.
vest_block1_desc: Headers verified against both Yespower and Argon2id, sharing one target.
vest_block2_desc: Yespower verification is removed from consensus code at this height.
vest_block3_desc: All headers validated using Argon2id proof-of-work exclusively.
vest_miner_body: No action is required. Because Yespower and Argon2id always shared the same target, mining software built on Argon2id continues to work unchanged before and after block 225,000. Light SPV wallets can rely on Argon2id alone for verification, since SHA-512 and Argon2id libraries are available on virtually every platform.
vest_pool_body: We do not use “delayed PoW” technology and are not affiliated with Komodo or its projects. Before block 225,000, Dpowcoin used a Dual PoW consisting of Yespower and Argon2id, where Argon2id itself is computed as two chained rounds salted with double SHA-512.
vest_pool_badge: Not affiliated with Komodo — no delayed PoW
dev_lead: Contribute to Dpowcoin Core or explore the RPC API.
about_intro: Dpowcoin Core is an open-source project that maintains and releases the Dpowcoin (DPC) client software. Based on Bitcoin Core, it provides both a full-node for fully validating the blockchain and a built-in wallet. Anyone is welcome to contribute.
about_team_note: This list will be expanded. We hope Dpowcoin Core will grow a large open-source developer community with many contributors to the codebase.
about_maintainers_body: Project maintainers have commit access and are responsible for merging patches from contributors. They perform a janitorial role — merging patches the team agrees should be merged — and act as a final check to ensure patches are safe and in line with project goals. The maintainers' role is by agreement of project contributors.
about_contributors_body: Everyone is free to propose code changes and to test, review, and comment on open Pull Requests. Anyone who contributes code, review, tests, translations, or documentation to Dpowcoin Core is considered a contributor. The release notes for each Dpowcoin Core release contain a credits section recognizing all who contributed over the previous release cycle.
about_bugs_body: Use the GitHub issue tracker to report bugs in the software.
about_community_body: Get help and discuss Dpowcoin with the community:
about_website_bugs_body: Found a problem on this website? Use the website issue tracker.
about_security_versions_body: Dpowcoin Core maintains security updates for the two most recent major release series. See the lifecycle schedule for currently supported versions:
about_security_report_body: To report a security issue, send an email to security@dpowcore.org — not for general support. Please follow responsible disclosure practices.
lifecycle_lead: Dpowcoin Core maintains security updates for the two most recent major release series. Older series reach End-of-Life (EOL) when a newer series supersedes them.
dev_build_intro: Build guides are maintained in the repository for all supported platforms.
brand_lead: Official Dpowcoin Core logos for use in media and documentation.
brand_usage_body: Use these assets to refer to Dpowcoin Core in articles, integrations, and applications. Do not alter the logo colors or proportions. Do not use the logo to imply endorsement.
brand_svg_note: SVG is resolution-independent and preferred for all uses.
dev_wp_intro: Technical specification covering Argon2id proof-of-work, LWMA-3 difficulty, monetary policy, and the genesis airdrop.
spec_pow_disclaimer: Dpowcoin Core is an open-source cryptocurrency implementing the Argon2id proof-of-work algorithm — ASIC-resistant, memory-hard, and built for fair mining.
about_support_body1: There is no pre-mine or developer fee in this coin. Servers for basic infrastructure and renewing the domain name still require payment to keep running.
about_support_body2: Donations are entirely voluntary — addresses are listed below.
about_support_warning: Check addresses carefully — only these belong to the developers.
about_support_thanks: Thanks to everyone who has supported us.
